clause_text: Delta Air Lines Accessible Travel Services Policy
Delta states: "We believe travel is for everyone. It's our priority to deliver the best service and ensure accessibility for all Delta customers."
Passengers can request help through My Trips, by calling 404-209-3434 (711 for relay services), or by completing an online Accessible Service Request form for travel more than 7 days away.

- Wheelchairs and mobility devices
- Dietary needs and allergies
- Service animals
- Cognitive and invisible disabilities
- Medical devices and portable oxygen concentrators
- Visual impairments (blind/low vision)
- Hearing impairments (deaf/hard of hearing)

Wheelchair assistance includes assistance with a push chair through the airport to gates. Electric cart service (available at select airports only) provides rides but cannot access different concourses or terminals.
Delta maintains specially trained Complaint Resolution Officers (CROs) at all airports to address disability-related concerns and ensure policies and procedures are properly implemented.
Delta Air Lines Baggage Policy
First Checked Bag: $45 USD each way for bags under 50 lbs (23 kg)
Second Checked Bag: $55 USD for bags under 50 lbs, each way
Weight and size restrictions specify that baggage size must not exceed 62 inches (158 cm) when you total LENGTH + WIDTH + HEIGHT.

- Delta SkyMiles Medallion Members receive complimentary first checked bag
- Select Delta SkyMiles American Express cardholders qualify for free first bag
- Active U.S. Military personnel have special allowances
- Diamond, Platinum, and Gold Medallion Members receive additional benefits
Passengers receive 1 Free Carry-On Bag and 1 Personal Item with checked bag purchase.
Standard products (Main, Comfort, Premium Select): 50 lb. limit per piece
context_sentence: Section: Weight Limits by Service Class

clause_text: Premium products: Delta One and First Class passengers follow the 70 lb. limit per piece.
context_sentence: Section: Weight Limits by Service Class

clause_text: Key West, Florida: All passengers traveling to or from Key West, Florida are limited to one checked bag.
Regional Jets to Mexico: No excess baggage (weight, size, or piece) will be accepted on regional service to Mexican cities.
Boxes: Boxes permitted only to Central/South America in original factory-sealed condition; prohibited to Brazil, Mexico, and Chile year-round.
Passengers can pay for standard checked bags fees with miles instead of paying in cash during the 24-hour check-in window on delta.com, the app, or at airport kiosks.
Delta Air Lines Cancellation and Refund Policy
Classic and Basic tickets are nonrefundable. Customers may cancel for an eCredit minus applicable fees. The eCredit remains valid for one year from purchase unless otherwise specified.
Customers with Extra or refundable ticket types can cancel before flight departure and receive a full refund automatically to their original payment method.

A cancellation fee will apply in most cases, unless you've purchased a refundable ticket type or experienced schedule changes, delays, or flight cancellations. Some Delta Main Classic and above tickets include waived change/cancellation fees within specific regions.

Tickets purchased directly through delta.com or the Fly Delta app within 24 hours of booking can be canceled for any reason and receive a full refund with no fees. Request must be submitted by midnight the day after purchase or departure, whichever comes first.

When flights are canceled or significantly delayed (3+ hours domestic, 6+ hours international), Delta attempts automatic rebooking. Customers unsatisfied with rebooking may request refunds or 5-year eCredits for unflown portions and upgrades.
Credit or debit card purchases are typically processed within 7 business days while other payment methods take approximately 20 calendar days.
Refunds cannot be issued for non-refundable tickets without disruptions, already-used tickets, or group reservations through travel agencies.
Delta Air Lines Carry-On Baggage Policy
Each passenger may bring 1 carry-on bag and 1 personal item free of charge to store items during flight.

Carry-on bags must meet these dimensions:
- Combined length + width + height of baggage must not exceed 45 linear inches (114 cm)
- Individual length, width and height measurements may not exceed 22" x 14" x 9"

Acceptable personal items include:
- 1 purse, briefcase, small backpack, belt bag, fanny pack, camera bag or diaper bag
- Laptop bags (computers cannot be checked unless directed by security)
- Similar-sized items fitting under the seat

The following carry on at no extra cost:
- A jacket and/or umbrella
- Food/drinks purchased after security
- Duty-free merchandise
- Strollers, wheelchairs, child safety seats, and assistive devices
Limit liquids, gels, aerosols and pastes items to a maximum of 3.4 ounces or 100 milliliters in one single quart-size, zip-top bag of clear plastic.

Delta specifically prohibits:
- Inflatable devices (wedges, cubes, beds)
- Knee defenders and seat recline blockers
- Foot hammocks and slings
- Window/tray table organizers, pods, tents, and full-head helmets
On Delta Connection flights with 50 seats or fewer, only personal items are permitted onboard; larger bags are gate-checked.
Delta Air Lines Check-In and Security Policy
Check in up to 24 Hours Before Your Flight through available methods including online, mobile app, airport kiosks, or in-person desks.

Passengers must present government-issued photo identification along with your boarding pass at check-in. International travelers need additional documentation, which can be provided via the mobile app.

Website/My Trips: Access check-in up to 24 hours before departure by selecting "Check In" in the page header or visiting My Trips. Users can enter confirmation number, SkyMiles number, or credit card number. Boarding passes can be printed or obtained digitally.

Fly Delta App: Download allows guest check-in or account login. Users who log in receive automatic check-in 24 hours before flight departure and access to Today Mode features including boarding pass and baggage tracking.

Airport Kiosk: Self-service option using SkyMiles, confirmation, or eTicket number for check-in, boarding pass printing, baggage addition, seat changes, service requests, and frequent flyer number updates.
Airport Check-In Desk: In-person assistance available at Delta desks with designated Sky Priority lanes for elite members and premium cabin passengers.
Curbside Check-In: Available at most U.S. airports with no fee (gratuities appreciated). Oversized baggage may require indoor processing.
Delta Air Lines Customer Service Plan
Delta's Customer Service Plan comprises 12 key commitments for flights to, from, or within the U.S.:

Lowest Fares
Delta discloses the lowest fare offered by Delta to the general public when available elsewhere, though they prioritize matching fares to your specific travel needs rather than always offering the absolute minimum price.

Delay and Cancellation Notifications
The airline provides status updates within 30 minutes of Delta becoming aware of the change through gate announcements, flight displays, reservations, delta.com, and subscription services.

Baggage Delivery
Delta commits to returning misplaced bags within 12 hours of arrival for domestic itineraries and 15-30 hours for international flights, with compensation for reasonable expenses from delays.

24-Hour Risk-Free Cancellation
Non-refundable tickets can be cancelled without penalty for 24-hours after the reservation is made for full refunds.

Timely Refunds
Credit card refunds process within 7 business days while cash/check refunds take within 20 days of Delta's initial receipt.

Disability and Unaccompanied Minor Accommodations
Delta provides wheelchair transport, enplaning assistance, accessibility services, and protections for children ages 5-17 traveling alone.

Essential Needs During Tarmac Delays
Food, potable water, operable lavatory facilities, and access to medical attention are provided when safety conditions permit.

Oversold Flight Procedures
Delta requests volunteers before involuntary denials, offers compensation via gift cards, provides rebooking on the next available flight, and supplies hotel accommodations if overnight delays occur.

Policy Disclosure
Refund policies, cancellation rules, loyalty program details, and aircraft information are available on delta.com and by phone.

Itinerary Change Notifications
Changes are communicated at least one hour before departure via contact information on file.

Complaint Resolution
Written complaints receive acknowledgment within 30 days and substantive responses within 60 days, with disability complaints resolved within 30 days.

Delay/Cancellation Mitigation
Rebooking on Delta or partner airlines occurs at no charge, complimentary hotel accommodations and meals/vouchers are provided for delays over 3 hours within Delta's control, and representatives have discretion to issue gift cards, travel credits, or miles.
Delta Air Lines Flight Change and Rebooking Policy

Domestic Travel (US, Canada, Caribbean):
Delta has simplified travel by eliminating change fees for tickets originating from the United States, Canada and the Caribbean to anywhere in the world (excluding Basic experiences).

International Travel:
Change fees range from $0-$400 depending on origin location and fare type for travel outside these regions.

Classic/Basic Non-Refundable Tickets:
Passengers may modify flights by paying applicable fees ($0-$400 based on route) plus any fare difference. The original Travel Experience tier remains unchanged.
context_sentence: Section: Ticket Types and Change Rules

clause_text:
Extra/Refundable Tickets:
Changes allowed without fees if fare rules permit and seats exist. Passengers pay only fare differences if new flights cost more.
context_sentence: Section: Ticket Types and Change Rules

clause_text:
Award Tickets:
No change fees for domestic U.S. travel or international travel originating in the U.S. and Canada. Changes permitted anytime before departure.
context_sentence: Section: Ticket Types and Change Rules

clause_text: Access modifications through My Trips online by selecting your flight, clicking "Change or Add Flights," choosing a new flight, and paying any required fare differences at checkout.

A $50 external handling charge applies to tickets purchased through travel agencies or partner airlines, except when changes occur via delta.com, are involuntary, involve military fares, or are same-day confirmations.
Reissued tickets must complete travel within one year from original purchase date.
Emirates Checked Baggage Policy
Emirates offers one of the most generous baggage allowances in the world. Baggage allowances are determined by either weight or piece concept, depending on your route and fare type.
Applies to all routes except those to/from the Americas and Africa.

Allowances by Class:
- Economy Special: 20 kg
- Economy Saver: 25 kg (30 kg from Australia/New Zealand)
- Economy Flex: 30 kg
- Economy Flex Plus: 35 kg
- Premium Economy: 35 kg
- Business Class: 40 kg
- First Class: 50 kg
Bag Limits: Multiple bags permitted up to total weight allowance; individual bags cannot exceed 32 kg.
Dimensions: Total dimensions (length + width + height) should not exceed 300 cm (118 inches). Dammam Airport limit: 215 cm.
Each bag has a fixed weight limit of 23 kg (Economy) or 32 kg (Premium/Business/First).

Baggage pieces allowed vary by fare type:
- Economy Special: One piece
- Economy Saver/Flex/Flex Plus: Two pieces
- Premium Economy and above: Two pieces
Dimensions: Individual bags limited to 150 cm (59 inches) total dimensions.
Oversize Baggage: Items exceeding 150 cm but under 300 cm incur additional fees; anything over 300 cm (or 215 cm from Dammam) cannot be checked.
Multiple Bags: Checking more than 15 pieces may result in delayed delivery on the next available flight within five days.
Skywards Members: Platinum/Gold/Silver members receive extra allowance on weight-concept routes.
Emirates Cancellation and Change Policy

Emirates permits booking cancellations through "Manage your booking" on emirates.com. Refund eligibility depends entirely on your ticket's fare conditions. The airline states: "Yes, you can receive a full refund as long as it's within the conditions of your fare."
Some tickets are non-refundable, while others may have cancellation charges applied. Passengers must review their specific fare conditions before cancelling.
Modifications are available online if your booking qualifies for changes. The policy notes: "You might be charged for certain changes, depending on the fare conditions of your ticket."

Key limitations:
- Online changes apply to all passengers simultaneously; individual passenger modifications require contacting Emirates directly
- Fare differences must be paid if the new itinerary costs more
- Partially used tickets may be eligible for changes based on fare conditions
- Changes must be completed 72 hours before departure for online processing
If modifications result in refund eligibility, passengers receive credit notes (EMDs) valid for one year. These can be applied to future Emirates travel or redeemed through a refund request form.
Chauffeur-drive services are cancelled if flights are changed and require new bookings 12-24 hours in advance depending on destination.
Frontier Airlines Baggage Policy
One personal item is included with your ticket.
Maximum dimensions are 14"H x 18"W x 8"D including handles, wheels, and straps.
Acceptable items include purses, totes, computer bags, briefcases, diaper bags, and kids' backpacks.
The size of your personal item will be checked during boarding.
Items larger than the allowed dimensions are subject to an additional charge.

Carry-on bags must not exceed 24"H x 16"W x 10"D and must weigh less than 35 lbs, including handles, wheels, and straps. Examples include large backpacks, small duffel bags, and small suitcases. Purchasing at initial booking offers the lowest prices.
This premium option guarantees overhead bin space and allows you to board in Group 1. To guarantee overhead bin space for one carry-on bag, customers must board with the Board First group in Group 1.
context_sentence: Section: Carry-On with Board First Option

clause_text: Checked bags can be purchased up to 60 minutes before domestic flights (60 minutes before international flights). Maximum size is 62 linear inches with a weight limit under 40 lbs. The service accommodates large duffel bags, large suitcases, and sporting equipment.
Checked bag weight can be upgraded to 41-50 lbs or 51-99 lbs through the website or mobile app for additional fees.
Frontier Airlines Contract of Carriage

The following terms and conditions as well as such additional terms and conditions presented on Frontier Airlines' website, fare rules, published schedules or printed on or in any ticket or ticket-less travel authorization apply to all tickets issued for travel on flights operated by or for Frontier Airlines, Inc. ("Frontier"), as well as that transportation, regardless of whether such ticket was sold by Frontier or its authorized agents or whether such ticket is used ("Contract of Carriage").
Codeshare - A marketing and business arrangement in which two airlines "share" the same flight (which might include connecting legs).
One airline places its designator code and flight number on a flight operated by the other airline, and markets and sells tickets for that shared flight as part of its published schedule.
Fare Rules - The rules and requirements associated with a ticket.

No-Show Cancellation - The automatic cancellation of a passenger's ticket upon such passenger failing to either (i) check-in for such passenger's flight, or (ii) board such passenger's flight, in either instance within the required times.
The automatic cancellation will apply to all subsequent flights, including return flights, on the itinerary.
Presentation of a ticket by someone other than the named passenger renders the ticket void and the ticket will then be treated as a No-Show Cancellation for all purposes of this Contract of Carriage.

Qualified Individual with a Disability - An individual with a disability who: (i) has a physical or mental impairment that, on a permanent or temporary basis, substantially limits one or more major life activities; (ii) has a record of such an impairment; or, (iii) is regarded as having such an impairment, as further defined in 14 CFR 382.5.
Standby Passenger - A passenger boarded subject to availability of seat space at departure time and only after all passengers having confirmed reservations for the flight have been boarded.

Ticket - The record of agreement, including electronic tickets, for passenger air transportation provided by the airline under certain terms and conditions to the passenger as described on the ticket, in the fare rules, and in this Contract of Carriage.

A. Frontier may refuse to provide transportation to any person and may require that a passenger leave an aircraft or be removed from an aircraft for the following reasons, in which case Frontier will provide a refund to the original form of payment of the amount paid for their ticket, which will be the limit of Frontier's liability:
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: No Seat for Safety Assistant
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: B. Frontier may refuse to provide transportation for the following reasons, in which case no refund to the original form of payment will be due:
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Government Direction
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Identification - The passenger refuses to produce a government-issued identification.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: MyFrontier Account; Card on File - The passenger refuses to create a MyFrontier account or have a credit or debit card on file with Frontier.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Failure to Check In or Appear
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Special Medical Requirements
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Qualified Individual with a Disability
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Prisoners - Frontier will not transport any prisoners under any circumstances.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Proper Attire - Any passenger who is barefoot and over 3 years of age, or who is not otherwise fully clothed.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Malodorous Condition
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Communicable Disease or Infection
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Refusal or Inability to Sit
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Failure to Follow Instructions
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Use of Ticket Issued to Other Person
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: Purchase in Violation of Contract of Carriage
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: C. Refusal to Sell Transportation - Frontier may refuse to sell transportation to any person.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: D. Customer of Size - If, in Frontier's sole judgment, a passenger is unable to sit in an aircraft seat without lifting either or both armrests and occupying all or a portion of the adjacent seats, or encroaching into the aisle or adjacent seats, the passenger will be required to purchase a ticket for an additional seat at the price then applicable. If sufficient, contiguous seats are not available, the passenger will be given the option to switch to flights on which such seats are available or be given a refund.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: E. Allergies (Peanut, Pet, or Chemical) - Items are not removed from the aircraft to accommodate a passenger's allergy. A "peanut-free" or "chemical-free" environment cannot be provided.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: H. Diversion While in Flight or Return to Gate - In the event that Frontier is required to divert an aircraft while in flight or return to gate because a passenger requires medical attention or due to the passenger's conduct, the passenger may be required to reimburse Frontier for the costs that Frontier incurs.
context_sentence: Section: 3 - Refusal to Transport and Special Conditions

clause_text: A passenger is entitled to transportation only upon presentation of a valid electronic ticket (e-ticket).
Paper tickets are not issued on Frontier ticket stock.
Tickets are honored only in the order in which they are issued.
Prohibited practices: Back to Back Ticketing, Throwaway Ticketing, Hidden City/Point Beyond Ticketing.
A ticket may only be used by the person named on the ticket.
Tickets may not be purchased for the transport of items of baggage.
A. Period of Validity
context_sentence: Section: 9 - Ticket Validity and Itinerary Changes

clause_text: Tickets issued by Frontier are valid for transportation only on the flights and dates shown on the ticket. If a passenger cancels a ticket before the scheduled flight departure time, the value of the ticket less any applicable cancellation fee and certain other carrier charges will be retained for 365 days from the date of cancellation of the ticket in the form of a refund travel credit. The refund travel credit has no cash value and may only be applied to subsequent tickets on Frontier flights for the same passenger.
context_sentence: Section: 9 - Ticket Validity and Itinerary Changes

clause_text: Except as required by law, Frontier shall have no obligation to reschedule passengers who cancel a ticket or to provide refund or credit for unused tickets.
context_sentence: Section: 9 - Ticket Validity and Itinerary Changes

clause_text: B. All tickets may be canceled within twenty-four (24) hours of the purchase and a full refund to the original form of payment will be given, except for tickets purchased for travel within 7 days (168 hours) of purchase. After that time, all tickets and optional charges are non-refundable to the original form of payment unless purchased as refundable or entitled to a refund pursuant to applicable law.
context_sentence: Section: 9 - Ticket Validity and Itinerary Changes

clause_text: Fares apply for transportation only between the airports for which they are published.
Fares are subject to change without notice until a ticket is issued.
Baggage fees apply to each checked bag.
Active U.S. military personnel and their accompanying spouse and children may check two bags at no charge. Overweight and oversize charges for the first two free bags are also waived.
B. Baggage Allowance Exceptions: Medical Assistive Devices, Wheelchairs, Essential Infant/Child Items - checked at no charge and do not count toward baggage allowance.
Standard baggage size: maximum of 62 inches (157 cm) in linear dimension. Standard baggage weight: maximum of 40 pounds (18.1 kilograms).
Baggage weighing 100 or more pounds (45 kilograms) or exceeding 110 linear inches will not be accepted, with the exception of assistive devices or musical instruments.

A. Liability Limited - Frontier will use reasonable efforts to transport passengers and baggage to the purchased destination, but published schedules, flight times, aircraft types, seat assignments, and similar details are not guaranteed and form no part of this Contract of Carriage.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: B. Force Majeure - In the occurrence of a force majeure event, Frontier may cancel, divert, or delay any flight without liability except to provide a refund for the unused portion of the ticket.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: Delay, Misconnection, or Cancellation - In the event a passenger's flight is canceled or significantly delayed or changed, Frontier will provide transportation on its own flights at no additional charge.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: Frontier will have no obligation to provide transportation on another carrier.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: Frontier may also offer a voucher, travel credit, or other form of compensation as an alternative to a refund.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: If the passenger does not accept alternative transportation, Frontier will provide a refund for the unused portion of the passenger's ticket including taxes and fees for ancillary services not provided.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: D. Involuntary reroute (diversion) - Frontier treats certain city groups as equivalent destinations (e.g., BWI/IAD/DCA/PHL; EWR/LGA/JFK; LAX/BUR/ONT/SNA/LGB; OAK/SFO/SJC; ORD/MDW/MKE). If diverted within a city group, Frontier has met its transport obligation and will issue no refund.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: E. Schedule Change Prior to Day of Travel - When a passenger's itinerary is changed due to Frontier schedule modification and is significantly delayed or changed, Frontier shall provide a full refund for the unused portion of the ticket unless the passenger accepts the changed flight or a voucher.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: F. Extended Onboard Ground Delays - Frontier maintains a separate Contingency Plan for Lengthy Tarmac Delays per FAA regulations.
context_sentence: Section: 17 - Failure to Operate on Schedule or Failure to Carry

clause_text: A. Voluntary - Passengers on an overbooked flight will be encouraged to voluntarily relinquish seats in exchange for alternate travel and Electronic Travel Voucher for future Frontier transportation valid 365 days.

B. Involuntary - If insufficient volunteers, passengers denied boarding will be provided transportation on next available Frontier flight. After boarding pass is scanned and passenger has boarded, removal is only for safety/security reasons.

C. Amount of Compensation:
- Domestic: New arrival within :59 = No Compensation; 1-1:59 hours = 200% (2x) one-way fare, max $1550; 2+ hours = 400% (4x) one-way fare, max $2150.
- International: New arrival within :59 = No Compensation; 1-3:59 hours = 200% (2x) one-way fare, max $1550; 4+ hours = 400% (4x) one-way fare, max $2150.
All refunds subject to government laws of the country where ticket was purchased/refund is made.
context_sentence: Section: 19 - Refunds; No-Show Cancellations and Service Charges

clause_text: First portion refunded is full amount of government-imposed taxes and fees.
context_sentence: Section: 19 - Refunds; No-Show Cancellations and Service Charges

clause_text:
No Use: refund equals fare plus ancillary purchases plus all taxes/fees.
   Partial Use: refund based on unused portion at lowest one-way fare for class of service paid.
context_sentence: Section: 19 - Refunds; No-Show Cancellations and Service Charges

clause_text: Refundable tickets: passengers should fill out online request at www.FlyFrontier.com.
context_sentence: Section: 19 - Refunds; No-Show Cancellations and Service Charges

clause_text: Tickets canceled within 24 hours of purchase (excluding those purchased within 7 days before travel): cancel online.
context_sentence: Section: 19 - Refunds; No-Show Cancellations and Service Charges

clause_text: Automatic Refund with automatic imposition of No-Show Cancellation Service Charge.
context_sentence: Section: 19 - Refunds; No-Show Cancellations and Service Charges

clause_text: The Service Charge equals the fare plus all ancillary purchases plus taxes/fees.
context_sentence: Section: 19 - Refunds; No-Show Cancellations and Service Charges

clause_text: Fares in US dollars (Canadian dollars for Canadian bookings).
context_sentence: Section: 20 - Currency and Mode of Payment

clause_text: All amounts due must be paid by credit or debit card; Frontier does not accept cash except at certain international locations.
context_sentence: Section: 20 - Currency and Mode of Payment

clause_text: Change Without Notice - Frontier reserves the right to amend this Contract without notice, provided no change applies to carriage already commenced.
No Class Action - Any case may be brought only in individual capacity, not as class or representative proceeding.
Time Limit for Action - No legal action may be brought unless commenced within 6 months from the date of the alleged incident.
Choice of Law - Governed by laws of the United States and the State of Colorado.
All right to trial by jury is irrevocably waived.
Southwest Airlines Contract of Carriage - Passenger (English Version)

(1) Reservations, purchase, ticketing, and transportation by Southwest Airlines Co. ("Carrier" or "Southwest Airlines" or "Southwest") are subject to this Contract of Carriage in effect on the earliest of the date on which the Ticket is reserved, purchased, or issued, and as amended through the date of travel, in addition to any terms, conditions, and restrictions applicable to your booking channel and included on any Ticket, except as otherwise provided within specific fare rules.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: The terms and conditions contained in this Contract of Carriage shall govern all published routes and services provided by Southwest as well as all fares and charges published by Southwest.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: This Contract of Carriage is subject to applicable tariffs on file with the U.S.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: Department of Transportation and laws, regulations, and rules imposed by U.S. or foreign governmental agencies; however, such tariffs, laws, regulations, and rules do not create any contractual or other obligations by Southwest that are owed to the Passenger, the Customer, or any other person or entity, or any right of action as against Southwest.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: If any portion of this Contract of Carriage conflicts with applicable laws, rules, or security directives from U.S. or foreign government agencies, the applicable laws, rules, or security directives shall govern.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: Southwest shall not be liable for any damage arising out of its compliance with any laws, government regulations, orders, rules, requirements, or security directives.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: By making a reservation, purchasing a Ticket, or accepting Carriage, the Passenger and Customer agree to adhere to, be bound by, and comply with all of the terms, conditions, and requirements set forth herein and applicable laws and regulations, including but not limited to federal laws protecting federal, airport, and air carrier employees who have security duties from assault and interference with the performance of their duties. Carriage offered by Southwest under this Contract of Carriage is subject to the Passenger's and Customer's compliance with these obligations, and a failure to comply shall constitute a material breach of this Contract of Carriage.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (2) Southwest Airlines reserves the right, in its sole discretion and to the extent not prohibited by law, to change, delete, or add to any of the terms of this Contract of Carriage without prior notice. All changes must be in writing and approved by an authorized representative of Southwest Airlines.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (3) If (i) this Contract of Carriage is amended after a Ticket is reserved, purchased, or issued but before commencement of travel, (ii) the amendment substantially affects the terms and conditions of a Passenger's Carriage, and (iii) the Passenger does not agree to be bound by the Contract of Carriage as amended, then the Passenger's sole recourse is to request a refund of the fare paid for unused travel. Such request for a refund must be made prior to the date on which travel was to commence.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (4) Southwest's obligations hereunder extend only to the Passenger, and as expressly set forth herein, to the Customer. In the event of a refund payment, Southwest may, in its sole discretion, refund all or a portion of the payment to the Customer but such payment shall not create a contractual relationship between Southwest and such third-party payor.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (5) This Contract of Carriage does not create, and shall not be construed to create, any right or cause of action based on any state, local, federal or international law, statute, regulation, rule, policy, guidance, treatise, convention, or other form of legal statements or requirements, and Southwest Airlines does not, in this Contract of Carriage, expressly or implicitly incorporate any such law, statute, regulation, rule, policy, guidance, treatise, convention, or other form of legal statements or requirements by reference.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (6) The Customer and Passenger waive any right of a travel insurer to exercise rights and remedies under this Contract of Carriage against Southwest with respect to any loss covered by a travel insurance policy.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (7) The invalidity of any provision herein shall not affect the validity of any other provision that shall remain in full force and effect.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (8) Unless specifically stated otherwise herein or where any limitation would expressly violate any applicable law, Southwest shall not be liable for any consequential, compensatory, indirect, incidental, or punitive damages arising out of or in connection with the performance of its obligations under this Contract of Carriage.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: (9) For Interline Transportation, please refer to Section 10 for additional information, terms and conditions.
context_sentence: Section: a. Application of Conditions of Contract

clause_text: Baggage means all luggage and contents contained inside, including but not limited to, suitcases, garment bags, tote bags, packages, camera and electronics bags, computer and equipment cases, briefcases, musical instruments, and similar articles, whether carried by the Passenger in the cabin or carried in the aircraft cargo compartments. Coats and wraps, when carried by the Passenger in the passenger cabin, will not be considered as Baggage.

Boarding Pass means a document (paper or electronic) issued by Southwest that bears the title Boarding Pass, the Passenger's first and last name, the flight number and date, the departure and destination airports, and a seat and boarding group assignment.
Carriage means the transportation of Passengers and/or Baggage by air, gratuitously or for hire, and all services of Southwest related thereto.
Checked Baggage means Baggage of which Southwest takes sole custody and for which Southwest has issued a baggage claim check and to which Southwest or Passenger affixed a baggage tag.

Customer means the person, agent, or entity who held a reservation or paid for, changed, or canceled a Ticket for the Passenger. For clarity, if the Passenger holds a reservation or purchases, changes, or cancels a Ticket, the Passenger is the Customer.

Flight Credit means a credit in a specified U.S. dollar amount, which has no cash value and may be redeemed solely for the purchase of Tickets. Flight Credits created from Tickets issued or changed on or after May 28, 2025, have an expiration date by which travel must be booked and flown. You may not transfer a Flight Credit for any reason unless expressly stated otherwise in this contract.

Force Majeure Event means any event outside of Southwest's control, including, without limitation, acts of God, and meteorological events, such as storms, polar vortex, rain, wind, fire, fog, flooding, earthquakes, haze, or volcanic eruption. It also includes, without limitation, a pandemic, public health emergency, catastrophe, government action, disturbance, or potentially volatile international condition, armed conflict, civil unrest or commotion, riot, embargo, war, or hostility, whether actual, threatened, or reported, strike, work stoppage, slowdown, lockout, or any other labor related dispute involving or affecting Southwest's service, mechanical difficulties by entities other than Southwest, Air Traffic Control, or the inability to obtain fuel, supplies, components, parts, fluids, airport gates, labor, landing facilities, or other items which Carrier, in its unilateral discretion, decides is reasonably necessary for the flight in question, or any fact or situation that was not foreseen, anticipated, or predicted by Southwest.

Significantly Delayed or Changed Flight means because of a Southwest-imposed flight delay or change:
A.

The Passenger is scheduled to depart from the origination airport three hours or more for domestic itineraries and six hours or more for international itineraries earlier than the original scheduled departure time;
B.

The Passenger is scheduled to arrive at the destination airport three hours or more for domestic itineraries or six hours or more for international itineraries later than the original scheduled arrival time;
C.

The Passenger is scheduled to depart from a different origination airport or arrive at a different destination airport;
D.

The Passenger is scheduled to travel on an itinerary with more connection points than that of the original itinerary; or
E.

The Passenger is downgraded to a lower class of service; or
F.
The Passenger is an Individual with a Disability and is scheduled to travel through one or more connecting airports that are different from the original itinerary.
(1) Confirmation of Reservations. A reservation on a given flight is confirmed by the issuance of a Ticket.

(2) Cancellation of Reservations.
(i) Passenger or Customer Initiated Cancellation Prior to Travel. If a Passenger or Customer cancels a Ticket at least ten (10) minutes prior to the scheduled departure time, eligible refunds will be provided consistent with Section 4.c. if affirmatively requested within one year from the date the Ticket was issued. If a Passenger does not travel and either the Passenger or Customer fails to cancel a Ticket at least ten (10) minutes prior to the scheduled departure time, the fare paid for such unused travel will be handled in accordance with the procedures specified in Section 2.a.(2)(iii).

(ii) Southwest has the right to cancel reservations (whether or not confirmed) due to the Customer's or Passenger's failure to comply with the rules set forth herein, including but not limited to, the failure to pay for the applicable Ticket under the conditions applicable to the fare for such travel and the failure to meet our check-in requirements as described in Section 5.

(iii) No-Show Policy.
(a) If a Basic fare segment on a Ticket is not canceled at least ten minutes prior to the scheduled departure time and the Passenger does not travel, then all segments associated with the Ticket are canceled, and the Rapid Rewards points and/or funds used to purchase the Basic fare segment(s), including taxes and government fees associated with those segments, are forfeited.
(b) If a Choice fare segment on a Ticket is not canceled or changed at least ten minutes prior to the scheduled departure time and the Passenger does not travel, then all segments associated with the Ticket are canceled, and Rapid Reward points and/or funds used to purchase the Choice fare segment(s), including the taxes and government fees associated with those segments, are forfeited.
(c) If a Choice Extra fare or Choice Preferred fare segment on a Ticket is not changed or canceled at least ten minutes prior to the scheduled departure time and the Passenger does not travel, all segments associated with the Ticket are canceled, and funds (including taxes and government fees) associated with the Choice Extra Fare or Choice Preferred Fare segment(s) are returned as a Transferable Flight Credit. Any Rapid Rewards points used to purchase the Choice Extra fare or Choice Preferred fare segment(s) will be returned to the account used to purchase the fare segment.

(vi) Prohibited Booking Practices.
(a) Fraudulent, fictitious, and/or abusive bookings violate Southwest Airlines rules.
Reservations made by Passengers or Customers, or Tickets issued to Passengers, must be made and issued only in respect of a Passenger's genuine travel requirements.
Reservations made to exploit or circumvent fare and Ticket rules are strictly prohibited.

(1) Transportation is subject to the fares and charges in effect when the Ticket is purchased. The fare is guaranteed once a reservation is purchased and a Ticket is issued. If a Ticket is purchased before an increase in the fare becomes effective, the Ticket shall be honored for transportation between the airports and at the fare for which it was purchased.

(2) Changes to any portion of a Ticket initiated by the Customer or the Passenger after its original issue will be subject to the fares, fare rules, tax increases, and charges in effect on the date the change is initiated. For these purposes, a change constitutes a change in flight number, origin, destination, intermediate points, flight date, flight time, or fare product. A Basic fare segment on a Ticket can only be canceled or upgraded to a higher fare product.
(4) All published fares and charges are stated in U.S. currency ("dollars" or "U.S. dollars").
(1) Basic fares and Choice fares are discounted, restricted, and nonrefundable fares.
context_sentence: Section: e. Basic fares and Choice fares

clause_text:
(1) No person shall be entitled to Carriage except upon presentation of a valid Ticket or proof of identification acceptable to Southwest to confirm that Carriage has been purchased. Such Ticket shall entitle the Passenger to Carriage subject to this Contract of Carriage and, in particular, certain terms and conditions as follows:
(i) Such Ticket is valid between the points of origin and destination via the specific routing designated on the Passenger's itinerary only.
(ii) The Passenger is in compliance with fare requirements.
(iii) The Passenger is in compliance with any other requirements of the Passenger's fare rules.
(iv) The Passenger's Ticket is in the Passenger's own name.
(v) The Ticket has not been altered or improperly issued.
(2) Tickets are Nontransferable. Tickets are nontransferable unless specified explicitly on the Ticket.
(4) Purchase of Adjacent Seat.
The purchase of more than one seat for use by a single Passenger is required in three situations.

First, the purchase of an adjacent seat is required when necessary to transport large musical instruments or electronic audio/video, medical, or otherwise sensitive equipment unsuitable for Carriage as Checked Baggage.
Second, the purchase of more than one seat for use by a single Passenger may sometimes be necessary to accommodate the pet of a Passenger with unique seating needs.

Third, the purchase of an adjacent seat is required for Passengers who, at the Carrier's discretion, cannot be safely accommodated in a single seat, whether because of a disability or because the Passenger would encroach upon the neighboring seat.

(1) Refundable Tickets. The fare paid for unused travel by Customers who purchase fully refundable, fare segments, including taxes and government fees, may, for any reason and upon surrender or cancellation of the unused fare segments, be refunded in accordance with Section 4.c.(3). A Passenger may instead choose to hold the funds as a Flight Credit or Transferable Flight Credit (depending on the fare purchased).

(2) Changes, Exchanges, and No Show. Ticket changes and exchanges within the same reservation will result in the initial Ticket being applied as the form of payment for the new Ticket. Southwest Airlines' unrestricted fares are fully refundable if canceled and then refunded instead of exchanging or changing your Ticket.
Southwest Airlines Customer Service Plan
The Southwest Airlines Customer Service Plan (SWA CSP) is issued by Southwest Airlines pursuant to 14 CFR § 259.5, and applies to flights operated by Southwest Airlines.
The CSP reflects Southwest Airlines' dedication to high-quality Customer Service, but is not a contract and does not create any contractual obligations on the part of the Carrier.
In addition, if there is any conflict between the language of the SWA CSP and the Southwest Airlines Contract of Carriage, the Contract of Carriage shall govern.
Our Senior Vice President & Chief Customer Officer bears the ultimate responsibility for our compliance with the SWA CSP.

Offering the lowest fare available
When you inquire about a fare or make a reservation on our website or mobile app, over the phone with a Southwest Representative, or at a Southwest ticket counter, we offer the lowest available fare for which you qualify at that time. For any of these channels, we will disclose that the lowest fare we offer might be available elsewhere if that is the case.

Notifying Customers of known delays and cancellations (including those related to diversions) for flights within seven days of departure
If your flight experiences a delay of 30 minutes or more or is cancelled, we use an automated system and will make reasonable efforts to notify you within 30 minutes after we become aware of such flight status change. Unless you opt-out, you will be notified by email or text, depending on the selection made at the time the reservation was booked. At the airport, including the departure gate and Flight Information Display screens under our control, we will make reasonable efforts to notify Customers of the updated status of the flight within 30 minutes after we become aware of such flight status change. Flight status information is also available on our website and by speaking with a Customer Representative at 1-800-I-FLY-SWA (1-800-435-9792).

Delivering baggage on time
We make reasonable efforts to load the items you entrust into our care onto the same plane you board and return them to you promptly at your destination. If delayed, we make reasonable efforts to return your luggage to you within 12 hours for domestic flights and within 15-30 hours for international flights.

If your luggage is delayed or lost for reasons outside of your control, you may file a mishandled baggage report at the airport and submit a claim for consideration of reimbursement of reasonable expenses you may have incurred. If you paid a baggage fee to Southwest and your checked bag was delayed 12 or more hours (for domestic itineraries) or 15 or more hours (for international itineraries) or was not recovered, we will refund the applicable fee(s) paid so long as you filed a mishandled baggage report.

Canceling your reservation(s) without penalty within 24 hours of booking
We allow you to cancel your unchanged reservation(s) without penalty within 24 hours of the initial booking for a full refund if the reservation is made one week or more prior to a flight's departure. The refund will then be processed to the form of payment of the ticket purchase.

When a refund is due, providing it promptly
Eligible refunds for tickets and optional travel fees are provided according to the original form of payment and the rules associated with that form of payment.

Refunds for eligible Southwest tickets and fees paid with a credit card will be credited back to the same credit card. When ticket or optional travel fee refunds are due pursuant to 14 CFR Part 260, we will make reasonable efforts to process credit card refunds within seven business days.

Refunds for eligible Southwest tickets and fees paid with cash will be issued by check. When a ticket or optional travel fee refunds are due pursuant to 14 CFR Part 260, we will make reasonable efforts to process refunds by check within 20 calendar days.

Disclosing refund alternatives
If we offer alternative transportation or a Flight Credit or Transferable Flight Credit in lieu of a refund, and if the Customer is eligible for a refund, we will advise the Customer that they are eligible for a refund. When we offer travel credits, vouchers, or other compensation, we will disclose any material restrictions, conditions, or limitations.

Flight credits for serious communicable disease
Customers must submit documentation as described in 14 CFR Part 262 that is acceptable to Southwest, disclosing the serious communicable disease. Customers who receive a nontransferable flight credit under this provision should contact Southwest for assistance with transferability. Our standard fare rules apply once a flight credit issued under this provision has been applied toward a new reservation.

Properly accommodating Customers with disabilities
Southwest Airlines will provide assistance to Customers with disabilities in accordance with the Air Carrier Access Act and the regulations implementing the Act under 14 CFR Part 382.

In the event that a Customer with a disability elects not to travel as a result of a carrier-imposed change in the Customer's itinerary that results in any of the following, Southwest Airlines will refund the unused fare of the Customer with the disability and any individual(s) in the same reservation as the Customer with a disability who does not want to continue travel without the Customer with a disability:
- The Customer with a disability is downgraded to a lower class of service, which results in one or more accessibility features needed by the Customer becoming unavailable.
- The Customer with a disability is scheduled to travel through one or more connecting airports that are different from the original itinerary.
- The Customer with a disability is scheduled to travel on a substitute aircraft on which one or more accessibility features available on the original aircraft needed by the individual is unavailable.

Meeting Customer needs during lengthy tarmac delays
Onboard delays are situations we always try to avoid. However, we have adopted the Southwest Airlines Tarmac Contingency Plan for situations when weather, gate-space limitations, visibility, airport conditions, mechanical problems, ATC requirements, or other uncontrollable circumstances cause a long onboard delay prior to takeoff or upon landing.

Handling "bumped" Passengers with fairness and consistency in the case of oversales, as required by 14 CFR Part 250
There may be instances where the number of Customers holding reservations exceeds the available seating capacity, resulting in an oversale. Southwest may attempt to take proactive steps to avoid an oversale situation. In the event of an oversale at the airport, our Customer Service Agents will ask Customers booked on the flight if they are willing to volunteer to take a later flight, typically in exchange for a voucher.

If we do not receive enough volunteers to accommodate all Customers who have purchased travel and have met our check-in requirements, we have to involuntarily deny boarding to Customers in accordance with our boarding priorities. If you are involuntarily denied boarding, you will be given a written Notice of Denied Boarding to help understand our policies, compensation, and travel alternatives. You will generally be entitled to compensation and transportation on the next available Southwest flight.

Disclosing cancellation policies, frequent flyer rules, aircraft seating configuration, and lavatory availability
Information about our refund policies, cancellation policies, frequent flyer rules, aircraft-seating configuration, and lavatory availability is available over the phone with a Southwest Representative or by following the links to Southwest.com.

Notifying Customers in a timely manner of changes in travel itineraries (more than seven days from departure)
We sell flights several months in advance, and at times, we may adjust our schedules. We will notify you as far in advance as practicable of any change to your itinerary, including routing, departure time, and/or arrival time. We will attempt to notify you within 48 hours of our becoming aware of the change.

You will have the option to select the revised itinerary, or, in the case of significant involuntary flight changes identified in 14 CFR Part 260, you may choose an alternate flight/date within a 14-day parameter of your original travel or cancel your trip without penalty and receive a refund in accordance with our Contract of Carriage.

Ensuring responsiveness to Customer complaints
Compliments, complaints, or questions about service? Email, call, or write to us. Written complaints will receive an acknowledgment in writing indicating receipt of the complaint within 30 days of receipt. You will also receive a substantive response no later than 60 days after our receipt of your complaint.

Identifying the services we offer to help mitigate inconveniences of Customers traveling on confirmed reservations during irregular operations
Southwest intends to operate flights as scheduled; however, there are situations that arise based on either uncontrollable and/or controllable circumstances that may cause a flight to be significantly delayed and/or canceled.

For flight delays of three (3) hours or longer that are within our control or Southwest-initiated cancellations that are within our control (e.g., mechanical problems, aircraft swap), we will rebook you on the next available Southwest flight(s) with seats available to your ticketed destination at no additional cost. During flight delays that are within our control of three (3) or more hours and/or Southwest-initiated cancellations that are within our control that result in a wait of three (3) or more hours for a flight at the airport, we will provide a meal voucher upon request at the airport for participating vendors within the airport or, if participating vendors and/or vouchers are not available, we will honor reasonable requests for reimbursement for meals purchased during such irregular operations.

If Southwest flight accommodations departing on the same day to your intended destination or applicable co-terminal city are not available following a flight delay or Southwest-initiated cancellation that is within our control (e.g., mechanical problems, aircraft swap), resulting in an unscheduled overnight delay or stay when your itinerary did not originally include an overnight layover, we will arrange lodging accommodations upon request if available, or will honor reasonable requests for reimbursement for lodging accommodations (provided you do not reside locally).

Effective for travel beginning April 30, 2024, for significant flight delays or Southwest-initiated cancellations that are within our control, you may be eligible for a fully transferable Southwest LUV Voucher (of at least $75) if the following conditions are met:
- Southwest canceled and/or delayed your flight within seven days of the scheduled departure; and
- You arrived at your final destination three or more hours after the scheduled arrival time; and
- You submit a request for the Southwest LUV Voucher via Southwest.com/DelayForm no later than one (1) year after the flight in question.

For flight delays of three (3) hours or more or Southwest-initiated cancellations that are not within our control (e.g., weather, Air Traffic Control, safety/security-related events, FAA-required crew duty limitations, infrastructure/utility problems), we will rebook you on the next available Southwest flight(s) with seats available to the Customer's ticketed destination at no additional cost.

General Policy:
Apple states that all transactions are final. However, the company provides limited remedies for delivery issues: If technical problems prevent or unreasonably delay delivery of Content, the exclusive and sole remedy is either replacement of the Content or refund of the price paid, as determined by Apple.

Apple reserves discretionary refusal rights: Apple may suspend or cancel payment or refuse a refund request if they find evidence of fraud, abuse, or unlawful or other manipulative behavior that entitles Apple to a corresponding counterclaim.

Automatic Renewal:
Subscriptions automatically renew until cancelled in the Manage Subscriptions section of the account settings. Users must act to prevent recurring charges.

Cancellation Timing:
For free trials, customers should cancel the Subscription at least twenty-four (24) hours before the free trial ends to avoid being charged.

Price Increase Notification:
Customers will be notified if the price of a Subscription increases and, if required, consent will be required to continue.

Subscription Termination Rights:
Apple reserves authority to cancel subscriptions if customers do not agree to material changes for which consent is required, or if Apple is unable to successfully charge the payment method to renew the subscription.

Apple addresses content removal scenarios: Content may be removed from services at any time (for instance, because Apple loses its right from the Content provider to make it available), after which it cannot be downloaded, redownloaded, or otherwise accessed from Apple.
The company disclaims ongoing availability guarantees: Apple has no responsibility to continue making Content available through its Services.

User-Generated Content Issues:
For problematic submissions, customers should report at reportaproblem.apple.com or by contacting Apple Support regarding materials violating submission guidelines.

Copyright Complaints:
Apple directs infringement claims to their Copyright Agent at copyrightnotices@apple.com or by mail to their Cupertino headquarters.
By placing an order at the Apple Store for Education, customers agree to the terms outlined below, along with Apple's Privacy Policy and Terms of Use.
K-12 Institutions: Employees of public or private K-12 schools, homeschool teachers, elected school board members, and PTA/PTO officers qualify.
Higher Education: Faculty, staff, and students of U.S. Higher Education institutions, plus parents purchasing for accepted or current students qualify.

Eligible customers may purchase:
- One desktop, Mac mini, MacBook, or display per year
- Two iPads per year
- One Apple Watch with education pricing per year
- Two accessories with education pricing per year
Purchases can be made online, in-store, or through authorized campus resellers, with combined quantities staying within limits.
iPhone Returns: Undamaged iPhones with accessories may be returned within 14 days for full refund. Customers remain responsible for wireless service agreements and associated fees.

Other Products: Undamaged items with original receipt and packaging can be returned or exchanged within 14 days of receipt. Returns by cash, check, or cash equivalent over $250 are refunded by check within 10 business days.
Exclusions: Electronic software downloads, subscriptions, gift cards, and developer products cannot be returned.
Gift Returns: Items returned with gift receipts receive Apple Store Gift Cards equal to the purchase price.
Software Returns: Sealed software with visible licenses cannot be returned unless unopened, with exception for Apple-branded software if licensing terms are rejected.

Apple reserves the right to adjust prices and correct errors. If prices drop within 14 calendar days of receipt, customers may contact Apple Care (800-393-399) for potential gift card compensation for the difference.
Purchases using international credit cards may incur exchange rate fluctuations and foreign conversion fees charged by the cardholder's bank.

Email confirmations acknowledge order receipt but do not constitute Apple's acceptance. Apple reserves the right to accept or decline orders for any reason. Cancelled orders after billing receive full refunds.

Risk of loss transfers when the carrier picks up items from Apple's warehouse. Customers receive shipment notification at this point. Apple cannot be liable for delays beyond its control but works to resolve delivery issues.

Customers or designated representatives may pick up orders with government-issued photo ID and order number. Apple notifies customers when orders are ready and may cancel unpicked orders. Third-party pickups require photo ID and order number; Apple is not responsible for third-party actions post-pickup.
Products are for end-user consumption only, not resale. Orders suspected of resale may be refused or cancelled. Online purchases ship only to U.S. addresses and comply with export control laws.

Apple may limit quantities available and reserves the right to change availability even after order placement. If products cannot be supplied after confirmation, orders are cancelled with full refunds.
Using Express Checkout indicates agreement to all sales terms. Users should disable Express Checkout on shared computers to prevent unauthorized purchases.
Apple Store Gift Cards are managed by Apple Value Services, LLC and apply only to Apple Retail Stores, Online Stores, or Contact Centers in the U.S.
They cannot be redeemed at iTunes, Mac App Store, resellers, for cash (except as legally required), or for international shipments.
Gift Cards cannot be resold.
Balance inquiries: 888-320-3301.
Apple audits education customer purchases for compliance.
Violations trigger charges to credit cards or invoices for the difference between education pricing and general public pricing.
If specific products weren't publicly available, charges reflect the closest equivalent product pricing.
Unpaid invoices may result in legal action with attorneys' fees owed by the losing party.
Apple enables customers to return undamaged products within 14 days of receipt with original receipt and packaging. For iPhones specifically, the return window is 14 days from purchase date.

Regarding refunds: If the item is returned within this timeframe, Apple will exchange it or offer a refund based upon the original payment method. For retail store returns involving cash transactions exceeding $250, refund checks are mailed within 10 business days.

The following items cannot be returned:
- Electronic software downloads
- Software-Up-To-Date subscriptions
- Apple Store Gift Cards
- Apple Developer Connection products
Gift purchases receive refunds as store gift cards rather than original payment methods. Devices with active security features may be refused unless disabled first.

Software returns have limitations: Opened software cannot be returned if it contained a seal with the software license on the outside of the package and you could read the software license before opening its packaging, with exceptions for Apple-branded software where licensing terms are rejected.

Products can only be returned in their original country of purchase. For iPhone returns, wireless account cancellation is not automatic — customers remain responsible for service agreements and related fees.
When Apple reduces pricing within 14 calendar days of product receipt, eligible customers may contact the Apple Care Center (800-393-399) to request a gift card for the price difference.
New and unopened products may be returned within 365 days with proof of purchase for a full refund. Open products can be returned within 180 days with proof of purchase for a full refund.
Refunds will be made in the same form of payment originally used to make the purchase.
Mattress purchases may be exchanged for another mattress one time within 90 days.
IKEA does not accept returns on plants, cut fabric, custom countertops, and as-is products. Items that have been modified, are dirty, stained, or damaged will not be refunded or exchanged.

- Receipt and valid government-issued photo ID are required
- Only the net purchase price shown on the receipt will be refunded
- Returns are not accepted at IKEA Planning Studio or IKEA Pick Up Point locations
When promotional items included in the original transaction are not returned, the value of the promotional item will be deducted from the refund amount.
Customers with damaged items should visit an IKEA store with the product and order confirmation, or contact IKEA US Customer Support at 1-888-888-4532 for replacement parts or resolution.

Continental U.S. (Excluding CO, HI & NV):
- Through 4/30/26: Free for IKEA Family members on orders $50+ (pre-tax)
- IKEA Family members pay $5 on orders under $50
- Non-members: Standard rates apply
- Minimum $35 merchandise subtotal required
- Maximum weight: 50 lbs
- No signature required
- Cannot deliver to PO Boxes or APO addresses

Colorado, Hawaii, and Nevada:
- IKEA Family: $5 for CO & NV; Hawaii starts at $80
- Non-members: $10+ for CO & NV; Hawaii starts at $80
- Same minimums and restrictions apply

Standard Delivery:
- IKEA Family: Starting at $19
- Non-members: Starting at $29
- Delivered within chosen date range (outside home or lobby)
- Orders under 300 lbs
- Special rates: CT, MA, NJ, RI, NY start at $39 (Family) / $49 (non-members)

Scheduled Doorstep Delivery:
- IKEA Family: Starting at $29
- Non-members: Starting at $39
- Choose your delivery date (as early as tomorrow)
- 4-hour notification window provided evening prior
- Special rates apply in select states and regions

Scheduled In-Home Delivery:
- IKEA Family: Starting at $59
- Non-members: Starting at $69
- Items delivered to one room of choice
- Mattress setup and packaging removal included

Express In-Home Delivery:
- IKEA Family: Starting at $69
- Non-members: Starting at $79
- Available as early as next day
- Selected zip codes only
- Mattress setup and packaging removal included

- Minimum $35 merchandise subtotal (after discounts, before taxes)
- Prices exclude tax
- Adult (18+) presence required at delivery (location exceptions apply)
- Orders over 50 lbs ineligible for small order shipping
- Orders over 500 lbs limited to In-Home delivery options
- Not all options available in all zip codes
- Cannot deliver to PO Boxes or APO addresses
- Delivery dates provided at checkout are estimates, not guarantees

- Available for $30 with in-store mattress purchases and delivery
- Free in CA, CT, OR, and RI (required by law)
- Service window: Within 30 days of delivery
- Currently unavailable for online mattress purchases
- Limited to certain zip codes within continental U.S.
Effective Date: May 1, 2026
These terms govern access to and use of IKEA North America Services' website, apps, and related digital services. Continued use indicates acceptance. Users under 18 cannot access the Services.
Users may establish accounts (IKEA Family, IKEA Home Shopping) by providing accurate, current information.
Account holders are responsible for maintaining confidential credentials and all activity under their account.
IKEA reserves the right to suspend accounts with inaccurate information.
Users must notify IKEA immediately of unauthorized access.
All content—including text, images, software, logos, and designs—belongs to IKEA entities and is protected by intellectual property law.
Users may download materials for personal, non-commercial use only with copyright notices intact.
Unauthorized copying, distribution, or modification is prohibited.
Users cannot use images without permission.

Services cannot be copied, republished, or distributed without written permission. Prohibited uses include: posting unlawful content, interfering with service operations, web scraping, reverse engineering, uploading malware, and unsolicited advertising. IKEA may remove violating content or suspend access at its discretion.

IKEA cannot guarantee product availability and reserves the right to discontinue items. Prices and availability are subject to change without notice and may vary across channels and locations. Quantity limits apply for regular customers.

A free loyalty club providing digital membership cards. Members can access offers in-store and online, track transactions, create shopping lists, maintain gift registries, and access receipts. See separate IKEA Family terms for full details.
A free loyalty program offering digital cards and exclusive benefits tailored to business customers. Members track transactions and access receipts online. Separate terms and conditions apply.
Digital tools for home improvement planning are for personal, non-commercial use.
Tools may be operated by IKEA or third parties.
Users must independently verify accuracy, suitability, and legal compliance before implementing plans.
IKEA disclaims all warranties regarding design accuracy, measurements, materials, and safety.

Members can scan items using the IKEA app and pay at dedicated checkout lanes. Eligible items include cash-and-carry articles and Swedish Food Market items; services and restaurant items are excluded. The service is free and subject to random verification.
IKEA attempts to provide complete, accurate information but makes no guarantees. Except as prohibited by New Jersey law, IKEA disclaims responsibility for errors or omissions in materials.

Users retain copyright to feedback and suggestions submitted, but grant IKEA a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free license to reproduce, adapt, modify, and distribute such content. This license permits IKEA to share content with partners and use it in service provision.

11.1 General UGC Terms: User-generated content includes ratings, reviews, questions, answers, photos, videos, and third-party platform content shared with permission. Submitters warrant they own intellectual property rights, are of legal age, and content doesn't violate terms. Content must not be false, infringe third-party rights, violate laws, contain hate speech, or include undisclosed compensation.

11.2 Ratings and Reviews: Users may rate store visits, products, or services after verified purchases or while logged into IKEA Family accounts. Reviews must reflect honest opinions based on actual experience and disclose material connections.
11.3 Questions and Answers: Users may voluntarily submit product questions or answers through IKEA's website, app, or in-store kiosks. Content must be accurate and based on actual experience.

11.4 IKEA Planning Tools: Users may submit photos, videos, or scans for design services. Content must be unaltered, not fabricated or deceptively manipulated, and users must have rights to record the depicted space.
11.5 Third-Party Platform Content: IKEA may request written permission to repost user content from Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, and similar platforms.
Permission requires responding with "#yesIKEA" and clear written consent.
Content becomes subject to these terms upon approval.
Users warrant content is accurate, depicts products for intended use only, and contains no unauthorized third-party depictions.

11.6 License and Consent: By submitting or authorizing UGC, users grant IKEA and affiliates non-exclusive, irrevocable, perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free rights to use content, including name, image, and likeness, in any medium without approval or compensation.
IKEA may remove content anytime.
Users waive moral rights and release IKEA from liability.
Content may be used to improve services, enhance customer experience, and train systems including AI summarization.

Users may request deletion under applicable law by contacting 1-888-888-4532; IKEA will respond within ten business days. Deletion doesn't affect prior uses, de-identified content, or aggregated data.
11.7 Miscellaneous: IKEA assumes no liability for UGC accuracy or substance.

Services may link to third-party sites over which IKEA has no control. IKEA doesn't endorse these sites and makes no representations about their accuracy or completeness. Users are responsible for reviewing third-party terms and privacy policies.
User information handling is governed by IKEA's Privacy Policy, incorporated by reference. Acceptance of these Terms constitutes acceptance of the Privacy Policy.

Within applicable law limits, IKEA is not liable for direct, indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages resulting from: service use or inability, information-based actions, password negligence, substitute goods costs, unauthorized access, improper authorization, or third-party conduct.
Services are provided "as-is" and "as-available." IKEA disclaims all warranties—express and implied—including merchantability and fitness for particular purpose.

In-Store Returns:
Bring items to any Nordstrom register. Refunds process in 3-5 business days.

Mail Returns:
Start return online to receive a prepaid shipping label. No printer needed—show your QR code at eligible USPS or FedEx locations. Processing takes 8-12 business days, with refunds following 3-5 business days after.

Nordstrom handles returns on a case-by-case basis with the ultimate goal of making customers happy. The company applies refunds to the original tender. Without a sales record, identification is required and refunds are issued as Nordstrom gift cards at current pricing.

- Gift cards cannot be returned
- Shipping charges are non-refundable
- No restocking fees apply
- There are no time limits for returns or exchanges
Fine Jewelry: In-store returns only with order confirmation or credit card used for purchase
Car Seats: Must be unopened in original boxes with unbroken seals
Balsam Hill: 30-day window or before December 25
Watchfinder & Co.: Pre-owned items are final sale and ineligible for refund or exchange
International Orders: Different return process; free returns don't apply
For best selection, order replacements online or call 1.888.282.6060.
Contact customer service at 1.888.282.6060.

Target Brand Items with Receipt:
365 days for returns

Most Items Sold by Target:
90 days for returns

General Items:
30 days for returns

Membership Benefit:
An extra 30 days added when you have a Target Circle membership or pay with a RedCard
Certain items have their own policies. Electronics and limited-time collections fall into this category. Product details pages specify individual return timeframes for these items.
The following cannot be returned: gift cards, trading cards, digital downloads, and unused breast pumps.
Drive Up: Use the Target app to initiate returns without leaving your vehicle
In-Store: Return items at any Target location with or without a receipt (use wallet barcode in app)
Mail-In: Send items back by mail at no cost
Large Items: Furniture and heavy electronics may qualify for return pickup services
Registry Items: Most purchases can be returned within one year of the event date through the registry dashboard
Recalls: Customers can view recalled items and follow necessary steps on Target's recalls page
The Zappos VIP program is free to join and offers members exclusive benefits including free expedited shipping and points-based rewards.
Shipping: Members receive FREE Expedited Shipping as a core perk.
Points System: The program operates on a straightforward earning model where customers accumulate points through purchases and activities.

- Purchase-based: Earn 1 Point for $1 Spent with the ability to redeem points for discount codes
- Login rewards: 5 points daily for logging into your account
- Product reviews: 10 points for reviewing purchased items
- Prime integration: Amazon Prime members can link accounts to earn an extra 1 Point per every $1 spent
- Brand promotions: Bonus points available during select brand promotional periods
Members can redeem points for $ off codes through the VIP dashboard.
Free Shipping Coverage: Zappos provides complimentary shipping anywhere in the United States, including territories and military addresses (APO/FPO).

Shipping Speed Options:
- Zappos VIP members get free expedited delivery
- Expedited shipping: 1-3 business day shipping (shipping in 3 business days or less) — unavailable outside continental US or to military addresses
- Standard shipping: 3-5 business day shipping (shipping in 5 business days or less)
Free Return Shipping: Returns are free from anywhere in the US, territories, and military addresses.

Return Window:
- Within 60 days: Full refund to original payment method or store credit
- After 60 days: Store credit available up to one year from purchase (original payment method refunds limited to 60 days); guest checkout customers only eligible within 60 days

Merchandise Condition Requirements:
Items must be returned in new condition and not be worn, used, altered, or washed with original packaging intact. Footwear must include the original shoe box undamaged. Final sale items cannot be returned.

Refund Processing:
Eligible refunds issue immediately upon carrier scan. Store credit processes within 24 hours. Financial institutions may require up to 10 business days; Afterpay up to 20 days; PayPal up to 30 days.

clause_text: Zappos grants customers a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license for personal shopping only. Commercial use, resale, data mining, and automated scraping are prohibited. The company reserves the right to refuse service and terminate accounts.

doc_type: Terms of Use / Customer Service Terms
clause_text: Users are solely responsible for maintaining password confidentiality. Customers must notify Zappos immediately of unauthorized account access. The platform does not serve minors under 18 without parental permission.

doc_type: Terms of Use / Customer Service Terms
clause_text: Title and risk transfer to customers upon carrier delivery. For returns, Zappos takes title only upon receipt at its fulfillment center. Refunds may be issued without requiring returns at the company's discretion.

clause_text: Zappos makes no warranty that product descriptions are 100% accurate, complete, reliable, current, or error-free. If products don't match descriptions, customers' sole remedy is returning unused items for refunds. The company reserves the right to cancel orders with incorrect pricing due to errors.

doc_type: Terms of Use / Customer Service Terms
clause_text: Disputes must follow mandatory binding arbitration, not class actions.

doc_type: Terms of Use / Customer Service Terms
clause_text: Customers must first send a formal Notice of Dispute and attempt 60-day good-faith negotiation.

doc_type: Terms of Use / Customer Service Terms
clause_text: Only after this period can arbitration proceed through JAMS.

clause_text: Small claims court remains available for eligible disputes.
